title: GPS Needs Einstein to Work
script_summary: How Einstein's theories keep your GPS accurate.

segments.voiceover: Without relativity, your GPS would drift by kilometers every day!
GPS satellites move fast and are far from us, causing time to act differently.
Special relativity says time slows down for moving objects—like satellites.
Meanwhile, general relativity states gravity affects time too—satellites are further from Earth's pull.
Together, these effects cause GPS clocks to gain time—up to 38 microseconds daily!
So next time you navigate, remember Einstein’s theories keep you on track.

Together, these effects cause GPS clocks to gain time—up to 38 microseconds daily! So next time you navigate, remember Einstein’s theories keep you on track.
